name: genvid-skills
description: Genvid reference skill pack — teaches any MCP-capable agent to drive the Genvid governed boundary.
migration_notes: 0.1.0 -> 0.2.0 (breaking): composition inference moved to the BYOK agent (#1667). The composition skills author structure themselves and cite verbatim screenplay quotes as content anchors instead of running the billable breakdown_* tools. storyboard_write compose payload is now Scene -> Beat -> Shot (a beats[] level was added between scene and shots), every shot carries provenance:{quote, scene?}, and scenes_write linked_assets items carry an optional provenance anchor; raw block_ids are rejected. breakdown_assets / breakdown_storyboard are no longer used by the pack's composition skills. boundary_compat narrowed from '>=0.1.0 <0.2.0' to '>=0.2.0 <0.3.0' (#1966) to match the pack version bump above, per the policy in README.md#compatibility. The range is now verified against the live boundary: scripts/boundary_client.py (#1992) reads the boundary contract version off the MCP `initialize` handshake (serverInfo.version, currently '0.2.0', deliberately bumped when the tool contract changes) and Gate 2 checks it against this range instead of skipping. 0.2.0 -> 0.2.1 (additive): new genvid-media-registration skill for the registered offline residency tier (#2025), driving the additive register_media boundary tool that landed in #2022. register_media is additive to the boundary contract (a new sibling of ingest_generated_media, no breaking change), so serverInfo.version stays '0.2.0' and boundary_compat is unchanged — this is a patch bump, not a minor. 0.2.1 -> 0.3.0 (breaking): the retired composition tools breakdown_assets, breakdown_storyboard, and sync_assets_with_screenplay were removed from the boundary (#1171) — Genvid funds composition inference on no surface. serverInfo.version bumps 0.2.0 -> 0.3.0 and boundary_compat narrows to '>=0.3.0 <0.4.0'. The pack's composition skills already author structure via the governed write tools (scenes_write / assets_write / shots_write / storyboard_write) and did not call the removed tools; the two skills that named them as billable examples (genvid-generator-connections, genvid-orientation) are reworded to the write-tool framing. 0.3.0 -> 0.4.0 (breaking): storyboard_write method='compose' now STAGES a pending review instead of writing live (#2139) — on a project that already has a production storyboard the composed result lands in *_pending + diff and is invisible to production reads until an Accept promotes it (POST /projects/{id}/storyboard/actions/approve). The compose response gains status ('pending_review' | 'applied') and diff_summary. A first compose onto an empty project still lands directly to production (status 'applied'). This is a breaking observable-behavior change for any agent flow that composed then read production expecting the write to be live, so serverInfo.version bumps 0.3.0 -> 0.4.0 and boundary_compat narrows to '>=0.4.0 <0.5.0'. insert is unchanged (post-certification additive change-order, applied live). Customer-facing SKILL.md wording (compose-goes-live -> compose-stages-review) rides the #2089-S3/#2110 re-sync wave and is applied under separate approval. 0.4.0 -> 0.5.0 (breaking): removed the write-triggered storyboard regen path (#2128) — the screenplay_write tool's regenerate_storyboard input field, which forced a full deterministic Fountain-parse re-derive of the storyboard on every governed screenplay write, is gone. Agent-owned storyboard_write(method="compose") is now the sole producer of a composed storyboard; create_screenplay/enhance_screenplay produce a screenplay only. serverInfo.version bumps 0.4.0 -> 0.5.0 and boundary_compat narrows to '>=0.5.0 <0.6.0'. insert/compose are unaffected. 0.5.0 -> 0.5.1 (additive, docs): genvid-orientation residency language updated for the residency-governance work (#2283/#2284): connected media is now creatable through register_media (storage_class='connected' + connection_name, hash-verified at registration, verified-tier from birth); organizations carry a residency posture (allowed_storage_classes / default_storage_class) that governs import surfaces; the registered->connected flip is an explicit human-approved storage_class update (connect_media tool) with hash verification, and connection removal is refused until dependent media are explicitly downgraded. Doc-only patch bump; register_media's connected form and connect_media are additive to the boundary contract, boundary_compat unchanged.
